items.title: An AI Val Kilmer is set to posthumously appear in a new movie
items.summary_auto: A year after the actor's death, a generative AI version of Val Kilmer will co-star in an independent film, in one of the boldest uses yet of artificial intelligence in moviemaking.

items.title: Rideau Cottage 'inadequate' home for a PM as decision on 24 Sussex looms: internal memo
items.summary_auto: Rideau Cottage, the historic red-brick house where Canadian prime ministers have been living for more than a decade, is inadequate and comes with security risks, according to a government memorandum prepared last summer.

items.title: Federal government to launch AI and Culture Advisory Council
items.summary_auto: The federal government announced a new advisory council to help it protect Canada’s creative industries from rapid advancements in AI, as the first National Summit on Artificial Intelligence and Culture wrapped up in Banff on Tuesday.

items.title: 'Should have shot him a couple more times': Ford congratulates person who shot alleged Ontario home invader
items.summary_auto: Ontario Premier Doug Ford congratulated a homeowner who shot and injured an alleged home invader in Vaughan, Ont., this week, saying intruders "need to be shot."

items.title: Pakistan and Afghanistan announce temporary pause in fighting
items.summary_auto: Pakistan and Afghanistan have declared a temporary pause in escalating fighting, two days after Kabul blamed Islamabad for a deadly airstrike in the Afghan capital that it said killed hundreds of people at a drug rehabilitation hospital.

items.title: Alberta premier says she took private flight last year following invitation from Saudi government
items.summary_auto: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith says she travelled on a private plane to Saudi Arabia last fall to meet with local government officials, following an invitation from a member of the Saudi royal family.

items.title: Nova Scotia teen arrested for allegedly planning school attacks with Manitoba youth
items.summary_auto: Police say a teen in the town of Bridgewater, N.S., and a youth from Manitoba have been arrested for allegedly planning simultaneous attacks at their local schools.

items.title: While Canada pumps the breaks on robotaxis, China hits the gas
items.summary_auto: Canada is still figuring out regulations when it comes to driverless vehicles, like robotaxis, on its roads. China, on the other hand, has embraced the technology, rolling out fully autonomous vehicles in select areas of cities like Beijing, Wuhan and Shanghai. CBC’s Lisa Xing and Chris Brown are in China as part of special coverage and took a ride inside a vehicle to see what it’s like.

items.title: Trump has delayed the Beijing summit. China wonders if he'll ever come to the negotiating table
items.summary_auto: The question echoing through Beijing’s diplomatic and political circles is whether U.S. President Donald Trump will show up for a summit in Beijing that was originally supposed to take place this month. Distracted by a self-launched war in Iran and facing mounting domestic pressure, Trump has pushed the China file to the side to focus on war strategy.

items.title: Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, saying war will boost global inflation
items.summary_auto: The Bank of Canada held its key interest rate at 2.25 per cent on Wednesday, saying that higher oil and gas prices from the war in the Middle East are likely to boost global inflation but that it's too soon to assess the conflict's impact on the Canadian economy.

items.title: Building owner facing 7 counts of manslaughter in connection with fatal Old Montreal fire
items.summary_auto: Émile Benamor, the owner of an Old Montreal building that caught fire in 2023, is being charged with seven counts of manslaughter and eight counts of criminal negligence causing bodily harm.

items.title: Mass cancellations of orthopedic surgeries likely within weeks over compensation dispute, doctors say
items.summary_auto: Edmonton orthopedic surgeons are warning of widespread surgery cancellations in the coming weeks, as a result of a April 1 change to how Alberta pays surgical hospitalists.

items.title: Child vaccination rate drops sharply in Michigan as RFK Jr. influences policy
items.summary_auto: Reuters data analysis shows swift fall in inoculations of toddlers. Officials link vaccination ​decline to Kennedy's policies and rhetoric.

items.title: Yellowknife resident says he's lost faith in the health-care system after repeated privacy breaches
items.summary_auto: A Yellowknife resident says he’s lost faith in the territory’s health-care system and wants more transparency after repeated breaches of his privacy. Such privacy breaches have happened hundreds of times over the past decade in the N.W.T., according to a government report.

items.title: Did organized criminals breach the Vancouver police property office? Court docs reveal VPD investigation
items.summary_auto: CBC News has learned Vancouver police are investigating whether someone with access to the force's property office colluded with organized criminals to tamper with key evidence in a high-profile murder case.

items.title: Sask. medical clinics impose fines, bans for no-show patients
items.summary_auto: Dr. Jason Hosain, who has studied no-shows in Saskatchewan and the response to them, said he understands the increased value placed on appointments with family doctors, even though he does not believe no-shows represent a growing problem.

items.title: Iran threatens attacks on Gulf oil infrastructure as Israel kills another regime official
items.summary_auto: Iran’s state television published a threat on Wednesday saying the Islamic Republic would be attacking oil and gas infrastructure in Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates amid a new wave of strikes on its Persian Gulf neighbours and Israel.

items.title: New program aims to support and connect 2SLGBTQ+ entrepreneurs in Canada
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Queer Chamber of Commerce has launched the FAM Program. It's aimed at providing networking, training and other supports to 2SLGBTQ+ entrepreneurs and businesses.

items.title: Israeli airstrikes kill at least 12 in central Beirut, Lebanese authorities say
items.summary_auto: Israel stepped up airstrikes on Beirut on Wednesday, killing at least 12 people and destroying a 10-storey building near the city center in the third week of its war with Hezbollah, Lebanese authorities said.

items.title: 'He got to do his race': Mother thanks Cain's Quest racers for helping late son cross the finish line
items.summary_auto: Jordan Anthony had always wanted to complete Cain's Quest, but tragically died months after his first attempt. As his teammate raced and completed the journey this year with his ashes, Anthony's mother says it's brought her closure in that her son's goal is now complete.

items.title: Border agency 'systemic collapse' allows man found guilty of immigration fraud to walk free and sue Canada
items.summary_auto: Gurpreet Singh is suing the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA), alleging it violated his charter rights. In 2022, he was found guilty of immigration fraud but a judge found CBSA treated him unfairly so he granted Singh a "windfall" — no criminal record and no jail sentence.

items.title: Canadian crypto fugitive is seeking U.S. presidential pardon on criminal indictment, U.S. lobbying firm says
items.summary_auto: A Washington, D.C., lobbying firm says it is seeking a presidential pardon for Canadian crypto fugitive Andean Medjedovic, documents filed with the U.S. Department of Justice show.

items.title: Diesel prices surge to highest since 2022, raising concern for shipping, consumer costs
items.summary_auto: The ongoing U.S.-Iran war is causing diesel prices to spike across the country, climbing nearly 30 per cent since the beginning of March. In some parts of Quebec, a litre of diesel is selling for almost $2.50.

items.title: Thunder Bay, Ont., long-term care home, staff member face criminal charges in resident's 2025 death
items.summary_auto: A city-operated long-term care facility in Thunder Bay, Ont., and a staff member face criminal charges following the death of an 86-year-old resident. Here's what we know about the investigation.

items.title: As Niagara Region’s only safe drug consumption site faces closure, advocates fear more people will die
items.summary_auto: Advocates fear the looming closure of the Niagara Region's sole safe drug consumption site, in St. Catharines, Ont., will result in a spike in overdose deaths and cases of blood-borne infections such as hepatitis C and HIV.

items.title: 'We've had enough secrets': First Nations group opposes Ontario move to limit freedom-of-information laws
items.summary_auto: A group representing First Nations is speaking out against Ontario’s proposed legislation to change freedom-of-information laws, saying there's concern the move would reduce Indigenous communities’ ability to access important information.

items.title: Top-ranked UBC looks to turn dominant season into 1st women's hockey championship
items.summary_auto: The University of British Columbia is the top seed among the eight teams vying for a national Canadian university women's hockey championship, which begins on Thursday.

items.title: Northwestern Ontario pleads for federal funding as highway fatalities mount
items.summary_auto: Northern Ontario politicians are urgently appealing to the federal government for help during one of the deadliest winters on record along 2,000 kilometres of highway between Kenora, Sudbury and North Bay.

items.title: As gas prices rise, ride-hail drivers feel especially pinched at the pump
items.summary_auto: Gas prices worldwide have soared since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran, and the widening war has pinched tanker traffic in the crucially important Strait of Hormuz shipping route. Ride-hail drivers, many of whom are on the road for hours a day, say they're being hit hard by the extra expense.

items.title: Science has an Epstein problem. Women in paleontology say it's a symptom of a deeper misogyny
items.summary_auto: The names of hundreds of scientists have appeared in the Epstein files, including prominent dinosaur researchers. As the paleontology community reckons with the fallout of these revelations, women in the field say it's bringing to the forefront what they've known for years — that paleontology is rampant with abuse and harassment, funding and fame are prioritized above all else and institutions protect established men at the top at the expense of younger women trying to build careers.

items.title: Self-harm among young Canadians is on the rise, specifically in girls, new research finds
items.summary_auto: A growing number of young people are turning to self-harm in Canada and other parts of the world, according to new research.

items.title: Lululemon's net income down as brand promises product shakeup
items.summary_auto: Interim co-chief executive and chief financial officer Meghan Frank said Tuesday that the apparel retailer plans to shake up its product assortment with fewer logos, a "more focused and co-ordinated" colour palette and a "more edited" assortment of small accessories.

items.title: Netanyahu posts video to dispel rumours of his death after disinformation spreads online
items.summary_auto: ​Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu posted a video of himself getting a coffee on Sunday in an apparent attempt to prove that online rumours of his death have been greatly exaggerated.

items.title: Cuba reconnects electrical grid, but millions are still without power
items.summary_auto: Cuba has reconnected its electrical grid across much of the country, the Energy and Mines Ministry said early on Tuesday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump escalated his rhetoric against the communist-run ‌island, saying he could do anything he wanted with Cuba.

items.title: Longest ballot organizers eye protest in upcoming Montreal-area byelection
items.summary_auto: A group that has signed up droves of protest candidates in a number of elections and byelections in recent years is organizing in another upcoming vote, this time in Terrebonne.

items.title: Is Iran waging war with mines in the Strait of Hormuz? | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains what we know about Iran's sea mine capabilities and why they can be such a destructive and difficult naval threat to remove — especially in a critical shipping route. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock, CAT-UXO and Getty Images
